questions.text: What is the primary advantage of a centralised oracle?

options.text: Speed and simplicity - one transaction, low gas, easy to implement.
No single point of failure.
Economic incentives for honesty.
Zero-knowledge proof verification.

questions.explanation: Centralised oracles are the fastest and simplest to set up, but they create a single point of failure.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Centralised oracles are the fastest and simplest to set up, but they create a single point of failure.

questions.text: How does Chainlink prevent a single malicious node from manipulating the final price?

options.text: By using median aggregation - the outlier is ignored as long as the majority is honest.
By banning the node forever.
By requiring all nodes to agree exactly.
By using a single data source for consistency.

questions.explanation: The median filter makes the feed resistant to outliers. A malicious node submitting a false price has no effect unless it controls the majority.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

The median filter makes the feed resistant to outliers. A malicious node submitting a false price has no effect unless it controls the majority.

questions.text: Which oracle model is best suited for verifying complex, private IoT data?

options.text: ZK Oracle (WASMify) - processes data off-chain, submits only a proof.
Centralised oracle.
Chainlink with 21 nodes.
Any oracle, they all handle private data equally well.

questions.explanation: ZK oracles can process large, private data off-chain and only submit a tiny proof on-chain - ideal for IoT, identity, and AI use cases.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

ZK oracles can process large, private data off-chain and only submit a tiny proof on-chain - ideal for IoT, identity, and AI use cases.

questions.text: Why is a decentralised oracle with 21 nodes all fetching from the same API still risky?

options.text: The API itself becomes the single point of failure - source-level diversity is as important as node diversity.
21 nodes are too many.
The gas cost would be prohibitive.
Chainlink doesn't support 21 nodes.

questions.explanation: Node diversity without source diversity is a false sense of security. All nodes relying on one API means that API is the single point of failure.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Node diversity without source diversity is a false sense of security. All nodes relying on one API means that API is the single point of failure.

questions.text: What is the primary reason MST's low fees change the oracle landscape?

options.text: Decentralised and ZK oracles become economically viable for frequent updates, which is impractical on expensive chains.
MST eliminates the need for oracles entirely.
MST provides free oracle services.
MST only supports centralised oracles.

questions.explanation: Sub-paisa gas fees mean that multi-node oracle updates and ZK proof verifications are affordable, enabling architectures that would bankrupt a project on Ethereum.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Sub-paisa gas fees mean that multi-node oracle updates and ZK proof verifications are affordable, enabling architectures that would bankrupt a project on Ethereum.

questions.text: "A decentralised oracle network like Chainlink eliminates all trust assumptions because nodes stake tokens as collateral."
questions.explanation: 1 mark: Correctly states FALSE. +1 mark: Justification mentions at least two residual trust assumptions (collusion, smart contract bugs, source manipulation) and contrasts with ZK oracles.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

1 mark: Correctly states FALSE. +1 mark: Justification mentions at least two residual trust assumptions (collusion, smart contract bugs, source manipulation) and contrasts with ZK oracles.

questions.text: "For a production DeFi lending protocol handling ₹100 crore in TVL, a centralised oracle is acceptable as long as the oracle server has 99.9% uptime."
questions.explanation: 1 mark: Correctly states FALSE. +1 mark: Justification addresses the trust model (hacking, bribery), downtime during critical periods, and the disproportionate cost-benefit of using a more robust oracle on a low-fee chain like MST.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

1 mark: Correctly states FALSE. +1 mark: Justification addresses the trust model (hacking, bribery), downtime during critical periods, and the disproportionate cost-benefit of using a more robust oracle on a low-fee chain like MST.

questions.text: A DeFi protocol needs an ETH/USD price feed updated every hour. Compare the annual oracle cost using (a) a centralised oracle, (b) Chainlink with 7 nodes, and (c) a WASMify ZK oracle - on both Ethereum and MST.
